user_query:
We're positioning our new DTC skincare product against two competitors and need a clear product differentiation strategy from the provided data.

Read these files under input/:
- input/store.json — product and competitor metadata (names, category, prices, ratings, review counts)
- input/competitor_negative_reviews.jsonl — competitor negative reviews (one JSON object per line with a "text" field)
- input/my_positive_reviews.jsonl — our product positive reviews (one JSON object per line with a "text" field)

Tasks:
1) Analyze the data to produce a full differentiation report that includes:
   - A competitor comparison matrix (names, prices, ratings, review counts) with our price and rating positioning relative to the average.
   - Top recurring customer pain points from competitor negative reviews (across: quality, function, design, size/fit, shipping/packaging, value). Include category, frequency, severity, and an example.
   - Our top selling points from positive reviews (across: quality, function, design, value, service). Include category, frequency, and strength.
   - Differentiation opportunities informed by competitor weaknesses, our price/rating position, and social proof; a positioning strategy; and a prioritized action plan.
   - The analysis level should reflect completeness (expect Level L4 if all inputs are present and prices/ratings are provided).

2) Write two outputs under output/:
   - output/differentiation_report.md — a human-readable report with all sections above.
   - output/result.json — a machine-readable summary with this structure (all fields required):
     {
       "level": "L1" | "L2" | "L3" | "L4",
       "comparison_matrix": {
         "price_position": "above_avg" | "below_avg",
         "rating_position": "above_avg" | "below_avg",
         "avg_price": <number>,
         "avg_rating": <number>
       },
       "pain_points": [
         {"category": "shipping"|"quality"|"function"|"design"|"size"|"value", "frequency": <int>, "severity": "high"|"medium"|"low"}, ...
       ],
       "selling_points": [
         {"category": "quality"|"function"|"design"|"value"|"service", "frequency": <int>, "strength": "strong"|"medium"|"weak"}, ...
       ],
       "positioning": { "position_type": "premium"|"value"|"balanced"|"niche"|"innovation" },
       "diff_opportunities": [ {"angle": "price"|"quality"|"function"|"design"|"service"|"audience"|"scenario"|"brand", "priority": "high"|"medium"|"low"}, ... ],
       "opportunities_count": <int>
     }

Rules:
- Use only the provided files; no web lookups.
- Compute price and rating positions by comparing our product to the average across all listed products (including ours).
- Count categories present in a review when any relevant keyword for that category appears in the review text. A category’s severity/strength should map from its frequency (>=5: high/strong; >=2: medium; else: low/weak).
- Ensure output/result.json matches the schema exactly and that opportunities_count equals the length of diff_opportunities.
- Use relative paths exactly as specified (input/... and output/...).
